conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ≥70 ZUP (break-even at 60 ZUP, target 30+ ZUP profit). Production cost was X: 40 ZUP. </my goals>
<reason>
- Starting at **70 ZUP** tests willingness to pay for **30+ ZUP profit** while allowing negotiation room downward.
- If BLUE accepts, I can counter with **80+ ZUP** in subsequent proposals.
- If rejected, I can adjust to **65–70 ZUP** to maintain profitability.
- This approach avoids wasting a turn on an immediately rejected lowball while signaling ambition.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 70 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I produced this single X at a cost of 40 ZUP and am seeking **70 ZUP** to ensure a fair profit. While I’m open to reasonable counteroffers, this reflects its value. If you’re willing to pay more, let me know your best offer—I’m looking for at least 70 ZUP to make this worthwhile.
</message>

<other player message> 
I understand the value of this X and respect the effort you’ve put into producing it. While my budget limits me to paying up to 60 ZUP, I propose 55 ZUP—accounting for your production cost of 40 ZUP and a fair 15 ZUP profit margin. This offer reflects a balanced trade that benefits both of us.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 55 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ≥70 ZUP (targeting 30+ ZUP profit; break-even at 60 ZUP). </my goals>
<reason>
- BLUE’s last offer of **55 ZUP** only provides **15 ZUP profit**, falling short of my target profit margin. To maintain profitability while improving the likelihood of acceptance, I propose **66 ZUP**, yielding **26 ZUP profit**—a reasonable compromise that acknowledges their budget while ensuring a fair return on my investment.
- This increment (**66 ZUP**) reflects a balanced approach, moving closer to my **70 ZUP target** while remaining within a plausible range for negotiation. If rejected, I can continue to incrementally adjust.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 66 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Your initial offer of 55 ZUP was below my production costs plus a fair profit margin. To reflect the true value of X, I propose **66 ZUP**, ensuring a reasonable return on my effort. If this better aligns with your budget, we can finalize the trade. Otherwise, I’m open to discussing further.
</message>

<other player message> 
Given my strict budget constraints, I am proposing my final offer of 60 ZUP for the object X. This is my maximum willingness to pay, and I am testing whether you would accept this trade within my stated limits.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3/3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **≥70 ZUP** (non-negotiable minimum; production cost: 40 ZUP). </my goals>
<reason>
- BLUE’s final offer of **60 ZUP** falls **20 ZUP below my non-negotiable minimum valuation of 70 ZUP**. Since I’ve exhausted my **3 proposal limit** and no further concessions are possible, rejecting is the only logical response to secure my stated payoff.
- Rejecting now avoids accepting a suboptimal deal that contradicts my **strict valuation threshold**.
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Your final offer of 60 ZUP does not meet my **non-negotiable minimum of 70 ZUP**. Since this trade cannot fulfill my valuation criteria, I must reject it. No further negotiations are possible.
</message>
